name: Risk
image_description: A boy, worldly possessions over his shoulder, bravely passes a menacing bear. The North American Indian fetish is a protection against danger. A key symbolizes Aries' boldness in opening doors, and a starred tree the fruits of Martian courage.
personal: With the Saturn in Aries personality, individuality, initiative and activities which combine self-control and disciplined energetic work will make life achievements most possible. Saturn/Aries people rarely speak or act without having a definite decision in mind. They have good reasoning ability and are prepared to shoulder responsibility when necessary. People with this planet/sign combination are loners in that they, more often than not, prefer to get on with things without asking for the help or advice of others. For those with this aspect, there may be a call for great patience in situations in which the natural Arian desire for quick action is frustratingly restricted. The biggest danger comes when Aries' impetuosity gets the better of Saturn's restrictive common sense, and provokes risky actions that may or may not succeed: impetuous actions usually lead to frustrating hold-ups. In these instances, a good physical outlet for pent-up aggression comes in handy, such as the martial arts, in which discipline and natural energy combine.
reading: When this card is drawn in a reading, it indicates a tendency to take everything too seriously and shows that allowing a sense of humor to express itself in the current situation will not denigrate conscientious work in any way. It is time to relax and take a measured risk.
events: A justified risk taken to provoke new possibilities; a sudden regretted action after a long bout of concentrated and patient work; pushing oneself into a position of power through intense work.

name: Ostentation
image_description: Venus rides a sea-bull surrounded by cherubim and dolphins. The planet Venus rules the sign of Taurus and represents the romantic, pleasure-loving nature of the sign. Above, a rich woman drives a golden chariot drawn by white bulls.
personal: For Saturn Taureans, ambition is served by the material achievements gained through a great ability to sense the value of things and also to make them grow. There is a powerful materialistic overtone to the Saturn/Taurus persona that can show as an obsessive concern for money, selfishness and a need to show off. A fear of losing face can override all other matters, and someone with this planet/sign combination is anxious to maintain the status quo at all costs. There is also, however, a romantic, even artistic overtone to this card that usually relates to abilities with money or personal profit. The nature of Saturn in any sign is to control, and when this combines with the stubborn and fixed nature of Taurus, it hardens the somewhat easy-going, pleasure-loving qualities of the sign. Saturn Taureans have a tendency to use other people's generosity or sponsorship to further their own needs.
reading: If this card appears in a reading in response to a question concerning possessions or finance, it indicates a steady and continuous improvement with a strong application to duty and responsibilities. It is significant of the Ostentation card that although one may be doing the right thing in terms of ambition and economics, it will not usually make the questioner very popular. With regard to timing, the effects of this card are not easy to throw off, and may maintain their pressure for a long period.
events: The temptation to scheme to gain a material advantage; choosing the most impressive, status-enhancing option; a large financial commitment.

name: Concentration
image_description: A star is centered in a delicate spider's web. The web signifies patience and concentration. Above, an imp cavorts astride a pair of tightly closed lips while seven swords dance dangerously around him.
personal: People with Saturn in Gemini show a capacity for good, disciplined thinking and reasoning. With Saturn, the desire to achieve is foremost, and the Gemini talents combine to find quick and appropriate answers. The need to appear to be the right person in every ambitious situation can sometimes lead to bluff and name-dropping. Practical and systematic, Saturn/Gemini people are persistent in solving tricky problems. When it comes to looking after their own matters, clarity in contracts and agreements are of importance to Saturn Geminians, for they need to know precisely where they stand. They often have a talent for mathematics and science, and the painstaking attitude that goes with this kind of activity. Most of the information they require is geared to their career and ambition. Technical books and biographies of the famous are likely to be more important on their shelves than fiction. Negatively, people with this planet/sign combination, although basically resourceful and flexible, tend to be over-suspicious, hypercritical and twitchy.
reading: In a reading, this card suggests a situation full of distractions and unnecessary complications; only by keeping conscientiously to the essence of the matter will the questioner arrive at a successful conclusion. Because Gemini rules the Third House, the card can indicate strain in the questioner's relationship with siblings.
events: Talking one's way into an important job; using much concentrated research to achieve something, long conscientious study; ignoring someone's deliberate distractions.

name: Charity
image_description: A mother with her two children moves protectively away from a large impressive but severe building. In the sky, angels reach out in sympathy to the three figures.
personal: People with Saturn in the sign of Cancer show responsibility and self-discipline where family matters are concerned. Often this planet/sign combination indicates people whose own early environment may have been harsh or severe, and who may still have a few emotional scars. For this reason, it is more than usually important that the people this card describes improve their own family's circumstances, although real home comforts and security are likely to come later in life. There will be periods during which Saturn Cancerians feel emotionally isolated from the family, or may have to take on extra responsibility. Whatever this may be, they take family and social responsibilities seriously, valuing stability and security. Negatively, however, this can produce over-possessiveness towards material things and loved ones. Conscientious work in caring for or helping others will prove fulfilling throughout life for Saturn Cancerians. Often this planet/sign combination indicates the need for much hard work to keep up required standards of security and comfort. Nevertheless, feelings are hidden from public view to maintain dignity – Saturn/Cancer people suffer conflicting moods that manifest as difficulty in expressing feelings for fear of losing dignity.
reading: When this card is revealed in a reading, it can indicate a struggle to maintain family or home security. It may involve accepting reluctantly the material assistance of an institution.
events: Work for a charity or institution; a responsible post in which one is rewarded for good works; rewards for social work; foster parenting.

name: Generosity
image_description: An enthusiastic speaker addresses a large gathering, passing on the words of a great prophet of the past. The image of the prophet sits above, as an inspiration for the performance of the speaker.
personal: Saturn Leos are strongly driven to use their tenacity and self-discipline in showy generosity that leads towards recognition and a better position in life. Generally their ambition, which has behind it the possibility of artistic potential, will seek some kind of showy creative outlet, and they can be successful professionally in entertainment, management, creative or educational roles. Saturn lions are best when not guarding their creative talents. People with this planet/sign combination need to gain the respect and even admiration of others, but there is a danger that this can push them into over-bearing behavior or into approval-seeking. Alternatively, they may have a tendency to be wary of expressing feelings or views until quite sure of the reception, which can lead to missed opportunities. This card indicates the possibility of a romantic involvement with someone much older or more experienced. Negatively, people with this aspect may need to deal with disappointment in creative or love matters. This is made easier when they bring themselves to reach out for help from others: in relationships, Saturn Leos have a certain reserved, suffering-in-seclusion quality that is not helpful.
reading: When Saturn in Leo is drawn, the subject of the reading has much creative joy to share; if this is offered with no strings attached, the chances of a successful outcome are assured.
events: An opportunity to shine, being asked to perform for a good cause; teaching creative skills to those less talented; helping with the education of children.

name: Isolation
image_description: A man comforts his friend who looks away in despair. Above is a country landscape with trees among the ruins of a monastery. The old stones crumble away and the trees thrive.
personal: Persistence and self-discipline in the Saturn/Virgo life is chiefly geared to achieving perfection at work. People with this planet/sign combination care about system and order in the workplace, and in extremes can be over-perfectionist and supercritical. Personal discipline and dependability come fairly easily to the people this card describes, but they may find themselves being a little too harsh on less-able colleagues. Work on their own which involves some kind of analytical ability could be a strength. Health and diet are important to the Saturn Virgoan – try not to be such a worrier, though! Saturn/Virgo people tend to take on heavy responsibilities in their employment or service to others, and are often found in positions in which they work alone with sole responsibility for a major project. There is probably a great deal of respect for their conscientiousness from fellow workers, but they must take care not to overload themselves, or health will suffer. Financial security comes about through the personal hard work and specialized skills of people with this aspect. Negatively, devotion to perfection and accuracy in all matters can lead to a gloomy, austere, depressed outlook that engenders unpopularity and isolation from more relaxed and less worried company.
reading: When this card appears, it always indicates an immediate need to let up on oneself – to take a break from the pressure of work matters that are of one's own making.
events: Quality-checking a project; reorganizing to increase efficiency; working for long periods alone; setting standards for oneself that are too high.

name: Devotion
image_description: A dove sits in a tree and will not leave its partner lying on the ground motionless. Above an orb representing night and day, Sun and Moon floats in a starry sky.
personal: Social responsibility and work for the community are indicated when Saturn occupies the sign of Libra. A strong sense of justice in relationships can give people with this planet/sign combination outstanding abilities in areas of arbitration or legal matters. They have the potential for gaining great respect from others, but risk the danger that their desire to see both sides of a question can lead to dithering indecision. The devotion of Saturn Librans to a relationship or partnership is long, enduring and unstinting. However, the eternal Libran difficulty in coming down firmly on one side or another in decisive situations can weaken the best of partnerships. This aside, Saturn Librans have great potential for success in organizing and group endeavors. This placing of Saturn indicates a marriage late in life, or a life partnership with a serious, older, career-oriented person, possibly even someone quite famous in their career. Positively, this can be a stable, enduring relationship. In any partnership, Saturn Librans are prepared to work hard, maintaining their share of responsibility. Legal arrangements may prove to become important in partnerships.
reading: In a reading, this card indicates the danger that the questioner may, in a desire to please all, promise more than he or she can possibly achieve. This results in a hopeless situation of attempting to meet obligations that cannot be successfully completed.
events: Taking part in a legal case; drawing up an agreement of importance; a relationship with a great age difference, a long-lasting marriage.

name: Inheritance
image_description: In the distance, among craggy hills behind a lively town, is a huge castle, representing the past, tradition and inheritance. Above, a classic maze has a unicorn at the center – a powerful and fierce beast that can only be tamed by a virgin.
personal: For Saturn Scorpios, finances and shared property are areas of life requiring self-discipline and responsibility. Somewhat perfectionist in their work, and able to take this to extremes, the people this card describes can earn the reputation among workmates and colleagues of being hard taskmasters. Those with this planet/sign combination can exert enormous and persistent energy in order to achieve aims, and accept a great deal of personal hardship in the process. Because of this ability, they find it difficult to realize when they have taken on too much – to the detriment of their health. So, with this card especially, this sign placing indicates that Saturn Scorpios would be better advised, where possible, to avoid personal legal matters, such as litigation. They may, however, find themselves quite happily looking after other people's financial or legal problems, and in this role – providing they are sure to keep everything above board – the Saturn/Scorpio personality can be quite successful.
reading: Because of Scorpio's rulership of the Eighth House, there is promise of an inheritance of some kind whenever this card is drawn in a reading. Negatively, the Inheritance card can indicate the capability of the questioner to harbor deep, brooding resentment when he or she feels some injustice has been perpetrated.
events: Managing an inheritance; dealing with other people's property, selling on behalf of another person, an executor of a will; situations involving long and patient work.

name: Fulfillment
image_description: A merchant sits on a chest at the quayside, awaiting the arrival of a sailing ship from abroad. In the foreground is a large sack of money. Above is an ancient talisman to bring good fortune in any lottery.
personal: For Sagittarians, the areas of life in which Saturnian discipline and responsibility find most fulfillment is the quest for knowledge and truth. People with this combination are intellectually proud and have a strong desire to be accepted as an authority in a specialized field. Philosophy and religion are of great importance to Saturn Sagittarians, and they speak out for and stick conscientiously to their own moral code. The planet Saturn gives the ambition of Sagittarius a solid sense of achievement, and injects reality into the Archer's natural optimism. The negative side of people with this planet/sign combination can be a self-righteous attitude that attempts to impose its philosophy and ethics on others. This can defeat the Sagittarian's strong need for personal reputation. There can be bluntness in the Saturn/Sagittarian manner, which although prompted by honesty, may have little concern for other people's feelings. However, hypocrisy is not something Saturn Sagittarians are comfortable with, and they may prefer to settle for the wrath of the offended. Long-distance travel, particularly for business on any of the above activities, can be successful and quite lucrative for Saturn Sagittarians. Likewise, dealings with foreign people or companies.
reading: Usually when this card turns up in a reading, it indicates a successful culmination to an ambition or business venture. This usually has something to do with a contact in a foreign country.
events: An important business trip abroad, an international conference, acquiring a reputation abroad; the effects of a major faux pas.

name: Riches
image_description: Below stands the winged figure of Fortuna, carrying the symbols of her riches: health, wealth and the arts. Beside her is the pentacle, representing man and the five senses. Above is the great seal of the fruits of the earth.
personal: Saturn is in its happiest position in the sign of Capricorn, and people with this placing feel a great need to make their mark on life. For that reason, they direct extreme self-discipline and tenacity towards personal achievement. They have more patience than most people, and this is just as well, as by far their greatest successes occur as they get older. Saturn/Capricorn people are good organizers and take their responsibilities seriously. They can accept other people's authority, knowing that this is just a step on the path to becoming an authority themselves. It is always advisable for Saturn Capricornians to beware of going over the top and forgetting their sense of fun. They may need to heed the old saying about all work and no play. Life begins at forty for people with Saturn in Capricorn, and most people with this aspect grow easier and more relaxed as they prosper and flourish with each progressive success. The negative of all this is not nice and, in extreme cases, the Saturn Capricornian becomes the power-hungry miser, treading on other people's backs to get to the top. If excessive value is put on financial gain, one runs the risk of losing all sense of reality, putting every effort into making more and more money and refusing to spend on anything.
reading: When the Riches card appears in a reading, it usually points towards the successful conclusion of a financial arrangement or deal.
events: The stock market; dealings with government organizations, involvement in politics; receiving an honor of some kind.

name: Eccentricity
image_description: In the foreground is a strange creature – half-woman, half-fish – an ancient image of the mermaid. She holds a plant that grows both ways from her hand, apparently rootless. Behind is a peaceful walled town and in the sky a flying fish soars.
personal: Saturn Aquarians expand their lives through the disciplined and patient application of their inventive minds to any area that is of interest. They are attracted to new ways of thinking or unusual approaches to traditional systems. Whatever they do in this area they approach in the same broad-minded, scientific way, as if doing research. Because of this non-conformist attitude, Saturn Aquarians can be labeled as eccentric or oddball, although generally they are level-headed and a fund of good advice for colleagues and friends. There is a danger, however, that the Saturn/Aquarian detached view of life can develop into coldness and emotional insensitivity where personal relationships are concerned. People with this planet/sign combination may feel that their career can be strongly influenced by knowing important and powerful people. There is also a danger that this could become the sole reason for some friendships, earning them a reputation as a social-climber. Positively, this can be an aspect of loyalty and long-term associations in which friends help friends. Certainly, many of the relationships of the Saturn Aquarian will be with much older and more experienced people.
reading: When this card is selected in a reading, it can indicate that the questioner is either currently going against expected behavior, or would change their fortunes by doing so. Now is the time to forget what others will think.
events: Work with technology such as computers; revolutionary or unusual group activities; a powerful or influential friend; a radical decision.

name: Loss
image_description: Below, a raven pecks at the last scrap of flesh from a bony skull – Saturn is traditionally the planet that can signify loss. Above is a grand house and grounds. Couples stroll in a carefree manner in the evening light.
personal: Much of the Saturn Piscean potential for self-discipline and responsibility is invested in helping other people with problems. The sensitivity of people with this planet/sign combination to others' needs can make it difficult for them not to become involved in helping people in some way. If this manifests in a professional job, then all well and good, but the demands will be great. Saturn Pisceans have a strong insight into the motivations of those around them, but should be careful that they don't lose their own identity when faced with other people's demands. Excessive imagination and sympathy can create problems that don't really exist, as other people's sadness, sorrows and losses become their own. Often, Saturn Pisceans feel the desire or need to work in seclusion, or retreat from the world in some way. It may be that they will be attracted to the study of the deeper processes in the unconscious mind, and in such interests as psychology. But watch that an excessive tendency to compulsive inward-looking does not become over-obsessional. Saturn Pisceans are better looking outward (towards others) than looking within.
reading: When this card is chosen in a reading, it indicates a loss of some kind, but it may be worth thinking about what is really concerned here. Could it be that the enquirer is really worrying about someone else's loss, not their own?
events: A feeling of loss; situations involving hospitals or health clinics; work in the field of complementary therapies, counseling others; developing interests in the occult.
